original: MLB record 50 runs for a doubleheader: Brooklyn Robins beat Philadelphia Phillies, 20-16 in first game, Phillies win 8-6 in nightcap, at the Baker Bowl in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
American basketball player John Sebastian makes 63 consecutive free throws while blindfolded
People's Republic of China launches 1st intercontinental rocket
Chung Kwung Ying does 2,750 "atomic" hand-stand push-ups
David Goch finishes swimming 55,682 miles in a 25-yd pool
USSR launches two cosmonauts to Mir space station
Arizona Diamondbacks' Randy Johnson becomes 16th pitcher to throw a perfect game (2-0 vs Atlanta)
Paul Dukas' symphonic scherzo "The Sorcerer’s Apprentice" (used in Disney's Fantasia film) premieres, based on poem byJohann Wolfgang von Goethe
Nicolas-Marie-Charles Barbier de la Serre, French soldier, invented the use of raised dots for writing (Louis Braille later used), born in Valenciennes, France (d. 1841)[1]
101-year-old Harold Stilson becomes oldest golfer to record a hole-in-one when he aces the 108-yard, par-3, 16th hole at Deerfield CC, FL
MLB Detroit Tigers pitcher Spencer Turnbull no-hits Seattle Mariners, 5-0 at Comerica Park, Detroit
DreamWorks Pictures "Shrek", starringMike Myers,Eddie Murphy, andCameron Diazin voice-over roles, debuts
Edmonton right wingJari Kurrisets a record for scoring in a Stanley Cup Finals game with 3 goals and 2 assists in a 7-2 win at Boston Garden
Jeana Yeager, American aviator (1st non-stop, non-refueled flight around the world), born in Fort Worth, Texas
Tropical Butterfly Garden at Cleveland Metroparks Zoo in Ohio opens
US Republican Party nominatesAbraham Lincolnfor president
Gene Roddenberrysuggests 16 names for Star Trek Captain; they include Kirk
Eruption of Mount St. Helens in Washington state triggers the largest landslide in history, killing 57 people and causing over $1 billion in damage
Bernard "Ben" Feringa, Dutch chemist, 2016 Nobel Prize for developing molecular machines, born in Barger-Compascuum, Netherlands
One of the largest-known fast-moving landslides forms Lower Slide Lake in Gros Ventre, Wyoming
Apollo 10 launches from Kennedy Space Center and later transmits the 1st color pictures of Earth from space
706 people called Kyle gather in Kyle, Texas, attempting to break the record for a gathering of people with the same name (record is 2,325 Ivans 2017)
Robert Tanner Freeman is 1st African American to graduate from Harvard Dental School
Tennessee Governor Ellington approves repeal of the Butler Act (or "Monkey Law") - prohibiting the teaching of evolution, upheld in 1925 Scopes Trial
Micky Ward beats Arturo Gatti by majority decision in a junior welterweight boxing bout in Uncasville, Connecticut; 'The Ring' Fight of the Year; first fight in their legendary trilogy
Gary S. Paxton [Larry Wayne Stevens], American musician and songwriter ("Monster Mash"; "Alley Oop)', born in Coffeyville, Kansas (d. 2016)
Diane Crump, American jockey (1st woman to race in Kentucky Derby), and hose trainer, born in Milford, Connecticut[1]
Omar Khayyám, Persian mathematician (cubic equations), astronomer (Jalali calendar, basis of the Persian calendar), poet (quatrains), and philosopher, born in Nishapur, Khorasan (now Iran) (d. 1131)
Dutch explorer Willem Barents sets off from Amsterdam on his third expedition to try to find a Northwest passage through the Arctic
Rhode Island enacts the first law declaring slavery illegal
Edwin Budding of England signs an agreement for the manufacture of his invention, a lawn mower. Saturdays are destroyed forever
Irish Music Festival first held in Dublin
New York Giants third baseman Bill Joyce becomes last player in baseball history to hit 4 triples in a game as Giants beat Pittsburgh Pirates, 11-5 at Exposition Park
Atkinson & Depeiaza take West Indies cricket team from 6-187 to 6-494 in a day v Australia
Dutch jurist and scholarHugo Grotiussentenced to life in prison in Loevestein Castle in the Netherlands (later escapes in a book chest)
Novelty song "The Streak" byRay Stevenshits #1

Final Indiana Jones film "Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny" premieres at the Cannes Film Festival, starringHarrison FordandPhoebe Waller-Bridge, directed by James Mangold and executive produced bySteven SpielbergandGeorge Lucas
Joseph Beer, Bohemian military trumpeter, clarinet virtuoso, and composer who revolutionized the clarinet by adding a fifth flap, born in Grünwald, Bohemia (d. 1812)
Margaret Ann Neve, British 1st recorded female supercentenarian (110 years, 321 days), born in Saint Peter Port, Guernsey (d. 1903)
Don Martin, American cartoonist "Mad's Maddest Artist", born in Paterson, New Jersey (d. 2000)
Mark Mothersbaugh, American new wave rock singer-songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist (Devo - "Whip It"), born in Akron, Ohio
Philadelphia Athletics rout Detroit Tigers, 24-2 at Shibe Park; Tigers use amateur players in protest ofTy Cobb's suspension from the game
Leon Shenandoah, Native American leader (Iroquois Confederacy), born in Hemlock Creek, New York (d. 1996)
11th Cannes Film Festival: "The Cranes Are Flying" directed by Mikhail Kalatozov wins the Palme d'Or
Beatles' last released LP, "Let It Be", released in US
American engineerRay Dolbyfounds Dolby Laboratories in London, England
Stanley Cup Final, Chicago Stadium, Chicago, IL:Henri Richardscores twice as Montreal Canadiens beat Chicago Black Hawks for a 4-3 series victory
K-pop boy bandBTSrelease their album "Love Yourself: Tear", sells 135,000 first week
Oliver Heaviside, English physicist (predicted ionosphere), born in London, England (d. 1925)
Vincent du Vigneaud, American biochemist (1955 Nobel Prize for Chemisty for work on cyclic peptide oxytocin), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1978)
"Big" Joe Turner, American blues singer ("Corrine Corrina"; "Shake, Rattle & Roll"), born in Kansas City, Missouri (d. 1985)
[Pierino] Perry Como, American singer ("Catch A Falling Star") and TV personality (The Perry Como Show), born in Canonsburg, Pennsylvania (d. 2001)
Margot Fonteyn, English ballerina considered the 1st lady of British Ballet (Giselle), born in Reigate, England (d. 1991)
Barry E. Jackson, American film production designer, animator, illustrator, graphic artist, and children's book author, born in Omaha, Nebraska
James Moginie, Australian rock musician, and songwriter (Midnight Oil - "Beds Are Burning"; "Blue Sky Mine"), born in Kalamunda, Western Australia, Australia
Rob Base [Ginyard], American rapper (Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock - "It Takes Two"), born in Harlem, New York
Erik Satie, Leonide Massine and Pablo Picasso's ballet "Parade" premieres in Paris
28.7 cm rain falls at Lake Maloya New Mexico (state record)
"If You Wanna Be Happy" by Jimmy Soul hits #1
East Germany and West Germany sign a monetary union treaty, effective July 1
